This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "letterThey", not "letter They"
issue.errorLine1: Composable functions that return Unit should start with an uppercase letter.\

issue.message: This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "classesSee", not "classes See"
issue.errorLine1: They are considered declarative entities that can be either present or absent in a composition and therefore follow the naming rules for classes.\

issue.message: This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "letterWhile", not "letter While"
issue.errorLine1: Composable functions that return a value should start with a lowercase letter.\

issue.message: This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "functionsSee", not "functions See"
issue.errorLine1: While useful and accepted outside of @Composable functions, this factory function convention has drawbacks that set inappropriate expectations for callers when used with @Composable functions.\

issue.message: This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "APIsSee", not "APIs See"
issue.errorLine1: Compose Material 2 (M2) is succeeded by Material 3 (M3). Please use M3 APIs.\

issue.message: This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "parameterSee", not "parameter See"
issue.errorLine1: This @Composable function emits content but doesn't have a modifier parameter.\

issue.message: This line continuation (**\**) should probably be preceded by a space character, otherwise this will render as a single word "valueSee", not "value See"
issue.errorLine1: This @Composable function has a modifier parameter but it doesn't have a default value.\

issue.message: No need to call `.trimIndent()` in issue registration strings; they are already trimmed by indent by lint when displaying to users. Instead, call `.indented()` on the surrounding `kotlin()` test file construction

No need to call `.trimIndent()` in issue registration strings; they are already trimmed by indent by lint when displaying to users. Instead, call `.indented()` on the surrounding `kotlin()` test file construction

org.jetbrains.uast.kotlin.KotlinLocalFunctionULambdaExpression is UAST implementation. Consider using one of its corresponding UAST interfaces: `UExpression`, `UAnnotated`, `ULambdaExpression`
org.jetbrains.uast.kotlin.KotlinUReturnExpression is UAST implementation. Consider using one of its corresponding UAST interfaces: `UExpression`, `UAnnotated`, `UReturnExpression`, `UJumpExpression`

issue.errorLine1: (it is UMethod || it is KotlinLocalFunctionULambdaExpression) && label == null ||
